mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing the present value of equity cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes over a 5-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate above 4.0% signals deteriorating household income and rising recession probability, triggering equity sell-offs and negative S&P 500 forward returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 4.0%, or the signal fails to predict direction with > 55% accuracy over 2020-2024.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and real GDP confirms robust aggregate demand that outpaces supply constraints, driving corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of RSAFS and GDPC1 fails to predict positive 60-day SPX returns with a Rank IC > 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle indicates manufacturing contraction and weakening economic momentum, causing short-term equity depreciation over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods produce positive 5-day SPX returns in > 60% of cases, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when FEDFUNDS is rising.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPIAUCSL reading above 310 signals persistent inflation that forces markets to price in prolonged restrictive policy, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in > 60% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 310, or the signal shows no directional edge over a 2018-2024 sample.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding labor demand and rising consumer spending capacity, which supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth fails to correlate with positive 60-day SPX returns, yielding a Rank IC below 0.03 across all 3-year rolling windows since 2010.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in housing starts and retail sales indicates a synchronized collapse in durable goods demand, signaling imminent economic slowdown and triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint declines in HOUST and RSAFS are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in > 65% of instances, or the interaction term shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in industrial production during a post-shock recovery regime signals resolving supply bottlenecks and improving profit margins, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during recovery periods fail to predict positive 20-day SPX returns, with accuracy dropping below 52% over a 5-year rolling window.
